plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate limits.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for income and stability.
Increase QQQ to 20% to boost broad index exposure.
Hold a significant cash buffer for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, focusing on capital preservation. Risk is significantly reduced by cutting leveraged ETFs and lowering concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL completely as it is flagged and leveraged.
Reduce SPAL to 2% to lower overall leveraged ETF exposure.
Add to CRWD, a high-scoring AI/Software name, for growth.
Buy TSLA, a top scanner setup, for diversified growth exposure.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while selectively adding to quality tech, aiming for moderate growth with controlled risk.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits, freeing up capital.
Sell SOXL completely as it is flagged below stop.
Add to SPAL to increase leveraged ETF exposure within limits.
Concentrate further in CRWD, a high-scoring AI/Software name.
Buy TSLA, a top scanner setup, for aggressive growth.
Watch NVDU for a potential future buy, given its high R/R score.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It uses and adds leveraged ETFs and concentrates in high-scoring setups, which significantly raises both upside and drawdown potential. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, and this approach can lose a lot fast if stops are not strictly followed.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and increase safety.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell CRWD to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Trim SMH to 10% to reduce high-beta sector exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income-focused distributions.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability and future income opportunities.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, focusing on parking cash for yield. It prioritizes low drawdown and reliable income by rotating into income/covered-call ETFs and broad index funds. Distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff, so this is not a savings account.
